device_description: Gula et al. 2010 reported seven years of experience using a video recording system to monitor endemic New Caledonian birds’ nests. Although the study was not specifically used for studying pollinators, the approach is readily applicable to monitoring invertebrate pollinators. They used commercial surveillance cameras with an infrared (IR) illuminator and electret mini microphones, both connected to a Digital Video Recorder (DVR) through a RCA cable. The system was powered by two 12 V 100 Ah batteries in parallel, located 50 m from the camera and microphone setup. Events of interest were manually scored, watching the footage at 24–36× speed. In the seven years of work, with seven similar systems, 22 000 h of monitoring were accrued; the longest continuously monitored period was 58 days. The total cost of each unit was quoted at € 520.
to_full_form: Yes, take me to the questions
device_construction: Modified off-the-shelf: Pre-built device with user-made adjustments or upgrades
device_billofmaterials: Gemini said  Sony 1/3-inch CCD infrared illuminator surveillance camera, Henri Electronic Electret mini microphone with built-in SMD amplifier, Panasonic NV-HV55 VCR or Archos 504/700 DVR, 100 Ah 12V deep cycle lead-acid battery, 6.4-mm multicore transmission cable comprising 3-mm video coaxial and 2.2-mm audio coaxial and two 2-mm power wires, 3M Temflex Rubber Splicing Tape, Archos A/V cable, Archos car lighter adapter and female socket or 12/220V transformer, plastic bag, adhesive tape, and battery clips.
device_mobility: Manually movable (limited): Can be repositioned by hand but not designed for frequent movement (e.g. repositioned tripod)
weatherproofing: Cameras are waterproof, tarp for batteries.
